questions: A user in Singapore has purchased a new Mac and wants to start using the built-in, on-device AI system integrated across compatible iPhone, iPad, Mac, Vision Pro, and certain Apple Watch models. Based on official Apple support and retail policies, explain: (1) the hardware and minimum operating system requirements across supported devices; (2) storage, language, and regional limitations that may affect feature availability (including any restrictions related to China mainland or the EU); (3) examples of major features available on macOS and how they differ, if at all, on visionOS and watchOS; and (4) the key return, refund, price-protection, and volume-purchase conditions that would apply if the device was bought from Apple’s official online store in Singapore. Provide a structured summary that integrates both the technical eligibility criteria and the applicable retail policies.

On Apple’s official support resources for macOS, several primary help sections are designed to onboard new users, assist switchers, highlight operating system updates, explain cross-device integration, and address security and intelligent features. Identify all the major support categories that (1) help someone set up a new Mac, (2) guide users transitioning from another operating system, (3) explain how a Mac integrates with other Apple devices, (4) outline features introduced in the latest macOS release named after a California location, (5) describe built-in privacy and security protections, (6) assist with recovering recently deleted credentials, (7) provide model-specific getting-started guides, and (8) introduce Apple’s integrated AI system. For each category, summarize the key capabilities or examples of functionality that are highlighted.

On the official support resources for Apple’s desktop operating system, there is an overview page that highlights several primary help categories designed to orient both new and existing users. Identify all the major user-focused help sections presented there, specifically those that: (1) assist brand-new users in setting up and personalizing their computer, (2) guide individuals transitioning from a different operating system, (3) explain integration with other devices in the same ecosystem, (4) introduce features of the latest operating system release, (5) detail built-in privacy and security protections (including credential recovery), (6) provide model-specific getting-started information, and (7) describe the platform’s integrated personal intelligence system. For each identified section, summarize its main purpose and the types of tasks or capabilities it covers.

A new Mac owner in Singapore wants to (1) troubleshoot issues like resetting account credentials, reinstalling the operating system, backing up data, and keeping macOS updated; (2) enable and use the built‑in on‑device artificial intelligence features across compatible Apple devices; and (3) understand their purchase protections if they bought the Mac through Apple’s official Singapore online store. Based on Apple’s official resources, identify the primary support channels available for Mac help, outline the hardware, software, language, storage, and regional requirements for activating and using these AI features (including cross-device conditions and key feature categories), and summarize the standard return window, refund conditions, price protection policy, and any major limitations that apply to purchases made in Singapore. Provide a structured explanation covering all three areas.

rounds.question: A user in Singapore has purchased a new Mac and wants to start using the built-in, on-device AI system integrated across compatible iPhone, iPad, Mac, Vision Pro, and certain Apple Watch models. Based on official Apple support and retail policies, explain: (1) the hardware and minimum operating system requirements across supported devices; (2) storage, language, and regional limitations that may affect feature availability (including any restrictions related to China mainland or the EU); (3) examples of major features available on macOS and how they differ, if at all, on visionOS and watchOS; and (4) the key return, refund, price-protection, and volume-purchase conditions that would apply if the device was bought from Apple’s official online store in Singapore. Provide a structured summary that integrates both the technical eligibility criteria and the applicable retail policies.
checklist_width: Compatible device models (e.g., M1 or later Mac; specified iPhone, iPad, Vision Pro, and Apple Watch models)
Minimum OS versions (e.g., iOS 18.1+, iPadOS 18.1+, macOS Sequoia 15.1+, visionOS 2.4+, watchOS 11+)
7 GB storage requirement (not required for Apple Watch)
Requirement to enable feature in Settings and keep device on Wi-Fi and power for model download
Language requirements (device and Siri language must match; list of supported languages)
Regional limitations (China mainland device/account restrictions; EU limitation for Live Translation with AirPods)
Examples of macOS features (e.g., Writing Tools, Genmoji, Image Playground, Smart Reply, summaries, Siri enhancements, ChatGPT integration, Focus features)
visionOS feature examples (e.g., Genmoji, Image Playground, Writing Tools, Smart Reply, summaries)
watchOS feature examples when paired with supported iPhone (e.g., Workout Buddy, Live Translation in Messages, Notification summaries)
14-day standard return window with receipt and original condition requirement
Non-returnable items (e.g., electronic software downloads, certain developer products)
7-day return window and 25% restocking fee for volume purchases (more than 4 units)
Price protection within 14 days of price reduction (excluding limited-time promotions)
Refunds limited to Singapore bank accounts and domestic remittance only

Official Apple Online Store in Singapore + Identified via Singapore-only shipping, domestic refunds, 14-day return policy, and 7-day/25% restocking rule for volume purchases
rounds.rationale: The question uses layered logic: it indirectly refers to Apple Intelligence by describing it as an on-device AI system spanning multiple Apple platforms (Deep), requiring the solver to identify the correct feature set and eligibility criteria. It also references Apple’s Singapore retail policies without naming the document, forcing identification of the correct regional store terms. The task then requires aggregating technical requirements, feature differences across platforms, regional restrictions, and detailed return and pricing rules from multiple sections (Wide).
source_nodes: How to get Apple Intelligence - Apple Support
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: To get started with Apple Intelligence features on your compatible iPhone, iPad, Mac, or Apple Vision Pro, update your device to the latest software version, and ensure you ha
rounds.answer_a:
To utilize Apple Intelligence, Mac users need a device with an M1 chip or later running macOS Sequoia 15.1 or newer [1]. Compatible iPhones and iPads require an A17 Pro (or newer) or M-series chip running iOS/iPadOS 18.1+ [1]. All compatible devices require a minimum of 4GB of free local storage. Initially, the device and Siri configurations must be set to U.S. English [1]. Regionally, Apple Intelligence is entirely unavailable on devices purchased in mainland China [2]. In the EU, Mac users can utilize the features natively, but iOS/iPadOS access is currently blocked due to Digital Markets Act (DMA) compliance unless tied to a non-EU Apple ID [2].

On macOS, core capabilities include system-wide Writing Tools, notification summaries, and a heavily revamped, context-aware Siri [1]. The experience differs significantly on peripheral operating systems: watchOS lacks native on-device AI processing and only surfaces Apple Intelligence via mirroring AI-summarized notifications from a paired iOS 18.1 iPhone [3]. For Vision Pro, Apple Intelligence is not available in current native visionOS releases, though future updates are planned to bring Mac-equivalent text and Siri capabilities [3]. 

Under Apple Singapore’s official online retail policies, customers have 14 calendar days from the date of receiving their Mac to initiate a return and receive a full refund, provided the device is in its original condition [4]. Singapore's price-protection policy allows consumers to request a refund for the price difference if Apple permanently reduces the cost of the purchased product within 14 days of delivery [4]. However, volume-purchase conditions strictly state that customers buying more than four identical items lose standard return privileges, and these bulk purchases are governed by distinct enterprise contracts rather than consumer retail terms [4].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. "How to get Apple Intelligence." Apple Support. https://support.apple.com/en-us/121115
[2] Apple Inc. "Apple Intelligence features and requirements." Apple Support. https://support.apple.com/en-us/119864
[3] MacRumors. "Apple Intelligence: Device Compatibility and Feature Limitations." https://www.macrumors.com/guide/apple-intelligence/
[4] Apple Inc. "Sales and Refunds Terms and Conditions - Apple (SG)." Apple Legal. https://www.apple.com/sg/legal/sales-support/sales-policies/retail_sg.html
rounds.answer_b:
## Apple Intelligence: Requirements, Features, and Singapore Retail Policies

### (1) Hardware & OS Requirements

Apple Intelligence requires **iPhone 15 Pro/Pro Max or later**, **iPad with M1 chip or later**, **Mac with M1 chip or later**, **all Apple Vision Pro models**, and **Apple Watch Ultra 2 or Series 10** (for select features). Minimum OS versions are **iOS 18.1, iPadOS 18.1, macOS 15.1, visionOS 2.1**, and **watchOS 11.1** respectively [1][2].

### (2) Storage, Language & Regional Limitations

Devices require at least **4GB of available storage** for model downloads. The device and Siri language must be set to a **supported language** (initially English variants, expanding to other languages over time) [1]. **China mainland** users face significant restrictions: Apple Intelligence is largely unavailable due to regulatory requirements. In the **EU**, certain features (like iPhone Mirroring and SharePlay Screen Sharing) have been delayed or restricted, though Apple Intelligence began rolling out in EU countries with iOS 18.4 [1][3]. Singapore users can access Apple Intelligence by setting language to supported English [1].

### (3) Feature Differences Across Platforms

On **macOS**, users get Writing Tools, Smart Reply, notification summaries, Image Playground, Genmoji, ChatGPT-integrated Siri, and Priority Messages [1]. On **visionOS**, core features like Writing Tools and Siri enhancements are available, but visual generation tools may be limited. On **watchOS**, only select features like notification summaries and Smart Reply are supported, with no image generation capabilities [2][4].

### (4) Singapore Apple Store Retail Policies

Apple Singapore offers a **14-day return/refund policy** from delivery for standard products in original condition. **Price protection** is generally not offered—Apple does not match post-purchase price drops. **Volume purchases** (particularly for business/education) may follow separate terms under Apple Business or Education programs. Personalized/opened software may be non-returnable [5][6].

## References
[1] Apple Support, "Apple Intelligence Requirements," https://support.apple.com/en-sg/120897
[2] Apple, "Apple Intelligence," https://www.apple.com/apple-intelligence/
[3] Apple Newsroom, "Apple Intelligence Available in More Countries," https://www.apple.com/newsroom/
[4] Apple, "watchOS 11," https://www.apple.com/watchos-11/
[5] Apple Singapore, "Returns & Refunds," https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
[6] Apple Singapore, "Sales Policies," https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/sales_policies
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identify the on-device AI system as Apple Intelligence and reference Singapore Apple Online Store retail policies. So both pass the core entity check.

Width/Completeness: Both fail multiple checklist items. Agent A incorrectly states 4GB storage (should be ~7GB), claims Vision Pro does not currently support Apple Intelligence (contradicts official support), and misrepresents volume purchases as losing return privileges entirely (instead of 7-day window with 25% restocking). It also omits key items like the Wi‑Fi/power download requirement, 7GB storage figure, supported language list, and Singapore-only refund limitations.

Agent B also states 4GB storage (incorrect), lists inaccurate minimum OS versions (e.g., visionOS 2.1), incorrectly claims price protection is generally not offered (contradicting Apple SG’s 14-day price adjustment policy), and omits the 7-day/25% restocking rule, refund-to-Singapore-bank limitation, 7GB requirement, and device setup requirements. It also provides incomplete regional nuance (EU/China specifics).

Both therefore contain significant factual inaccuracies across technical eligibility and retail policy sections (WIDE failures), though neither fails the core entity (so not DEEP alone, but BOTH due to multiple major detail errors).

Presentation: Agent B is more structured and readable, but because both contain substantial factual errors—including incorrect storage, OS, and retail policy details—accuracy issues dominate. 

Conclusion: Low-quality tie. Both responses suffer from multiple material inaccuracies across required checklist dimensions.

rounds.question: On Apple’s official support resources for macOS, several primary help sections are designed to onboard new users, assist switchers, highlight operating system updates, explain cross-device integration, and address security and intelligent features. Identify all the major support categories that (1) help someone set up a new Mac, (2) guide users transitioning from another operating system, (3) explain how a Mac integrates with other Apple devices, (4) outline features introduced in the latest macOS release named after a California location, (5) describe built-in privacy and security protections, (6) assist with recovering recently deleted credentials, (7) provide model-specific getting-started guides, and (8) introduce Apple’s integrated AI system. For each category, summarize the key capabilities or examples of functionality that are highlighted.
checklist_width: Set up new Mac: wallpaper customization, changing common settings, starting to use apps
Switching from Windows: file transfer from PC, learning macOS navigation
Integration with other Apple devices: view/control iPhone from Mac, copy and paste between devices
Latest macOS (Tahoe): new design, make phone calls from Mac, enhanced Spotlight actions, automatic translations in calls and messages
Privacy and security: built-in tools, password management, private browsing
Recover deleted credentials: recover a recently deleted password or passkey
Model-specific guide: Getting Started guide for a specific Mac model
Apple Intelligence: personal, private AI integrated into macOS
checklist_depth: Get started with your Mac + focuses on initial setup and basic personalization
Switched from Windows to Mac + explicitly for users transitioning from Windows PCs
Use your Mac with other Apple devices + emphasizes cross-device ecosystem features
What’s new in macOS Tahoe + tied to the latest macOS version named after a California location
Control your privacy and security + dedicated to built-in protection features
Recover a recently deleted password + specific credential recovery function
Get to know your Mac + provides model-specific Getting Started guides
Apple Intelligence + AI system described as personal, private, and integrated into macOS
rounds.rationale: The question uses categorical logic (onboarding, switching, OS updates, ecosystem integration, security, AI) to mask the exact section titles (Deep reasoning). It then requires aggregating distinct functional details from all identified sections—spanning setup, migration, device continuity, OS features, privacy tools, credential recovery, model guides, and AI integration—ensuring broad information synthesis across multiple support categories (Wide coverage).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Get started with your Mac You can set up and start using your new Mac in no time. Choose a wallpaper for your desktop, change common settings, start using apps and more. Learn
rounds.answer_a:
Apple’s official Mac support portal features distinct categories to help users navigate and maximize their desktop experience:

• **Set up your Mac**: Guides users through initial onboarding, utilizing Setup Assistant and Migration Assistant to seamlessly transfer existing files, applications, and system preferences to a new machine [1].
• **New to Mac**: Assists users migrating from Windows PCs, offering step-by-step instructions on the Windows Migration Assistant, familiarizing them with Trackpad gestures, and explaining Mac keyboard equivalents [1].
• **Mac and your other devices**: Details Continuity features that integrate Apple's ecosystem, highlighting cross-device capabilities like Universal Control, AirDrop, Handoff, Universal Clipboard, and the new iPhone Mirroring [2].
• **macOS Sequoia**: Outlines features introduced in the latest California-themed operating system release, such as easy automatic window tiling, detailed Safari Highlights, and enhanced video conferencing viewer controls [1] [3].
• **Security and privacy**: Explains built-in systemic protections, including FileVault data encryption, Touch ID secure authentication, advanced app privacy permissions, and built-in malware defenses [1].
• **Passwords app**: Introduces the new centralized credentials manager, guiding users on securely storing passkeys, Wi-Fi passwords, and restoring accidentally trashed items from the "Recently Deleted" folder within 30 days [3].
• **Mac Essentials**: Provides comprehensive, model-specific hardware manuals (e.g., MacBook Pro or iMac Essentials) covering external ports, Magic accessories, and device-specific workflows [4].
• **Apple Intelligence**: Explains Apple’s native artificial intelligence system, highlighting new capabilities like system-wide Writing Tools for summarizing text, Genmoji generation, and a redesigned Siri with richer contextual understanding [1] [3].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. "Mac Support - Official Apple Support." Apple, 2024. Available: https://support.apple.com/mac
[2] Apple Inc. "Use your Mac with other Apple devices." macOS User Guide. Available: https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/use-your-mac-with-other-apple-devices-mchl1d734309/mac
[3] Apple Inc. "What’s new in macOS Sequoia." macOS User Guide. Available: https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/whats-new-in-macos-mchl9d1b6cf1/mac
[4] Apple Inc. "Mac - Apple Support Manuals." Apple. Available: https://support.apple.com/manuals/mac
rounds.answer_b:
Apple's official macOS support page organizes help into several major categories [1][2]:

**1. Set Up Your Mac:** Guides users through initial configuration, including signing in with Apple ID, migrating data, choosing settings, and connecting to Wi-Fi during first startup [1].

**2. Switch from PC to Mac:** Helps Windows users transition by explaining how to transfer files, find equivalent apps, learn macOS navigation, and adapt to keyboard shortcuts and Finder [1][2].

**3. Continuity (Mac + Other Devices):** Highlights cross-device integration features like Handoff, Universal Clipboard, AirDrop, Sidecar (using iPad as a second display), iPhone camera as webcam (Continuity Camera), and Universal Control [1][2].

**4. What's New in macOS Sequoia:** Named after a California landmark, this section covers new features such as window tiling, iPhone Mirroring on Mac, Safari enhancements, gaming improvements, and updated productivity tools [1][2].

**5. Privacy and Security:** Describes built-in protections including FileVault encryption, Gatekeeper, Privacy settings for app permissions, Safari tracking prevention, passkeys, and Mail Privacy Protection [1][2].

**6. Recover Deleted Passwords:** Assists users in retrieving recently deleted credentials through the Passwords app or System Settings, where deleted passwords are stored temporarily before permanent removal [1][3].

**7. Mac Resources by Model:** Provides model-specific getting-started guides for MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, iMac, Mac mini, Mac Studio, and Mac Pro, covering hardware features unique to each device [1].

**8. Apple Intelligence:** Introduces Apple's integrated AI system offering Writing Tools, Smart Reply, image generation, enhanced Siri with on-screen awareness, notification summaries, and context-aware assistance [1][2].

## References
1. Apple Support, "macOS – Official Apple Support," https://support.apple.com/macos
2. Apple Support, "macOS Sequoia," https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/welcome/mac
3. Apple Support, "Recover deleted passwords on Mac," https://support.apple.com/guide/passwords/
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identified the major Apple Support categories (Set up your Mac, Switch from Windows, Use with other Apple devices, What’s New, Privacy & Security, Recover deleted passwords, Model-specific guides, Apple Intelligence). However, both failed a key checklist requirement: the latest macOS version named after a California location should be macOS Tahoe, but both responses incorrectly cited macOS Sequoia. This is a factual miss on a core sub-point.

Second, Width/Completeness: Both covered all eight required categories and provided relevant feature examples aligned with the checklist (migration tools, cross-device continuity, credential recovery, AI features, etc.). Aside from the incorrect OS version and features tied to it, coverage was strong and comprehensive.

Third, Presentation & UX: Both used clear formatting with bold headers and structured lists. Agent B’s numbered layout is slightly more scannable, while Agent A provides marginally richer feature detail and one additional citation. Overall, the user experience is comparable.

Because both agents share the same significant factual error regarding the latest macOS release, neither can be rated higher. Thus, this is a Low-Quality Tie due to a shared checklist failure.

rounds.answer_a:
On Apple’s official Mac support portal, several primary help categories are tailored to onboard, orient, and assist users:

• **Set up your Mac**: Guides brand-new users through initial configuration, personalizing system settings, and using Setup Assistant or Migration Assistant to safely transfer data from an older computer [1]. 
• **New to Mac**: Specifically assists individuals transitioning from a Windows PC by explaining operating system differences, introducing Mac keyboard shortcuts, and familiarizing users with basic gestures and desktop navigation [1] [2].
• **Mac and your other devices**: Explains how macOS integrates seamlessly into the Apple ecosystem using Continuity features. It highlights tasks like sharing screens with iPhone Mirroring, copying text via Universal Clipboard, sharing files through AirDrop, and using Universal Control [1] [2].
• **macOS Sequoia**: Introduces the newest features of the latest operating system, detailing capabilities such as streamlined window tiling, improved Safari browsing, and enhanced video conferencing tools [1] [3]. 
• **Security and privacy**: Details systemic protections including Touch ID, FileVault encryption, and application privacy controls. It features the new centralized Passwords app, which secures credentials, passkeys, and Wi-Fi networks, while allowing users to recover accidentally deleted passwords within 30 days [1] [3]. 
• **Mac Essentials**: Provides model-specific hardware manuals (like MacBook Air or iMac Essentials) that explain external ports, trackpad functionality, and device-specific workflow shortcuts to help users get started [4].
• **Apple Intelligence**: Describes Apple’s integrated AI system built into the platform. This section highlights machine-learning capabilities, including system-wide Writing Tools for text summarization, Genmoji generation, and a redesigned Siri that offers richer contextual awareness [1] [3].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. "Mac Support - Official Apple Support." Apple. Available: https://support.apple.com/mac
[2] Apple Inc. "New to Mac." Apple. Available: https://support.apple.com/explore/new-to-mac
[3] Apple Inc. "What’s new in macOS Sequoia." Apple. Available: https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/whats-new-in-macos-mchl9d1b6cf1/mac
[4] Apple Inc. "Mac - Apple Support Manuals." Apple. Available: https://support.apple.com/manuals/mac
rounds.answer_b:
Apple's official macOS support page presents several major help categories designed to orient users [1][2]:

**1. Set Up Your Mac:** Assists new users with initial configuration, including Apple ID sign-in, data migration from previous devices, Wi-Fi connection, personalization of settings, and first-time startup guidance [1].

**2. Switch from PC to Mac:** Guides Windows users transitioning to macOS by explaining file transfer methods, equivalent application alternatives, Finder navigation, keyboard shortcut differences, and adapting to macOS workflows [1][2].

**3. Continuity (Use Mac with Other Apple Devices):** Covers cross-device ecosystem integration including Handoff, Universal Clipboard, AirDrop, Sidecar for using iPad as a secondary display, Continuity Camera for using iPhone as a webcam, and Universal Control across devices [1][2].

**4. What's New in macOS Sequoia:** Introduces the latest release features such as window tiling, iPhone Mirroring on Mac, Safari improvements, enhanced gaming capabilities, and new productivity tools [1][2].

**5. Privacy, Security, and Credential Recovery:** Details built-in protections like FileVault encryption, Gatekeeper, app permission controls, Safari tracking prevention, passkeys, and Mail Privacy Protection. Also covers recovering recently deleted passwords through the Passwords app or System Settings [1][3].

**6. Mac Resources by Model:** Provides hardware-specific getting-started guides for MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, iMac, Mac mini, Mac Studio, and Mac Pro, highlighting unique features per device [1].

**7. Apple Intelligence:** Describes Apple's integrated AI system featuring Writing Tools, Smart Reply, image generation, enhanced Siri with contextual on-screen awareness, notification summaries, and personalized assistance capabilities [1][2].

## References
1. Apple Support, "macOS – Official Apple Support," https://support.apple.com/macos
2. Apple Support, "macOS Sequoia," https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/welcome/mac
3. Apple Support, "Recover deleted passwords on Mac," https://support.apple.com/guide/passwords/
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identified the official macOS support overview and mapped the seven required sections (setup, switchers, cross-device use, new OS features, privacy/security with credential recovery, model-specific guides, and Apple Intelligence). No core entity failures.

Width/Completeness: Both covered all checklist elements. Agent A slightly mislabels some sections (e.g., “Mac Essentials” instead of the more accurate model-based getting-started framing) and provides slightly less detailed task coverage in some categories. Agent B more explicitly aligns each section to the query constraints (e.g., clearly naming switching guidance, credential recovery, and model-specific resources) and includes richer examples like Gatekeeper, Sidecar, and Continuity Camera.

Presentation & UX: Agent B has clearer scannable headers, stronger alignment with the query wording, and more consistent descriptive depth per section. While Agent A includes citations, they do not materially enhance usability here. Both are well formatted, but B is more polished and comprehensive in a search-engine-like way.

Conclusion: Both are accurate and high quality, but Agent B provides a slightly better user experience through clearer structure and fuller coverage. Therefore, B_BETTER.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: macOS Support Reset your Apple ID password If you forgot your Apple ID password, you can reset it and regain access to your account. Learn how to reset your password Reinstall
rounds.answer_a:
For a Mac owner in Singapore, Apple provides comprehensive support, advanced integrated features, and specific purchase protections.

**Support and Troubleshooting**
Primary support channels include the official Apple Support website, phone support, live chat, and in-person Genius Bar appointments at Singapore retail stores [1]. Users can reinstall the operating system or reset account credentials utilizing the built-in macOS Recovery environment [1]. For data security, Time Machine handles local system backups, while macOS operating system updates are managed directly through System Settings [2].

**Apple Intelligence Capabilities**
To utilize on-device AI categories—such as system-wide Writing Tools, improved Siri contextual capabilities, and image or Genmoji generation—users must meet strict specifications. The Mac must feature an Apple Silicon M1 chip or later [3]. It requires macOS Sequoia 15.1 or newer and a minimum of 4GB of available storage [3]. Additionally, the device and Siri languages must initially be set to a supported English variant (e.g., English US) [3]. For cross-device synchronization and functionality, all compatible Apple hardware must be signed into the same Apple Account [3]. While fully supported in Singapore, Apple Intelligence faces severe regional operation limitations in mainland China and the EU [3].

**Singapore Purchase Protections**
Macs purchased through Apple’s official online store in Singapore include a standard 14-calendar-day return window starting from the delivery date [4]. To qualify for a refund, specific condition guarantees must be met: the device must remain undamaged and be returned alongside its original packaging, cords, and included accessories [4]. Furthermore, a strict price protection policy is available: if Apple systematically reduces the retail price of the Mac within 14 days of purchase, buyers can claim a refund for the difference [4]. Major limitations apply regarding non-returnable items, which completely restrict refunds on opened software, electronic software downloads, Apple Print Products, and Apple Gift Cards [4].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. "Mac Support - Official Apple Support." Apple. Available: https://support.apple.com/mac
[2] Apple Inc. "Back up your Mac with Time Machine." Apple Support. Available: https://support.apple.com/en-sg/104984
[3] Apple Inc. "How to get Apple Intelligence." Apple Support. Available: https://support.apple.com/en-sg/121115
[4] Apple Inc. "Returns & Refunds - Shopping Help." Apple (SG). Available: https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
rounds.answer_b:
**1. Primary Mac Support Channels**

Apple provides several support avenues for Mac troubleshooting. The official Apple Support website (support.apple.com/mac) offers guides for resetting passwords, reinstalling macOS via Recovery Mode, backing up with Time Machine, and updating macOS through System Settings [1]. Users can also access the Apple Support app, contact Apple via phone or chat, schedule Genius Bar appointments at Apple Store locations, and use Apple's community forums. For Singapore specifically, Apple Authorized Service Providers supplement in-store support [1][2].

**2. Apple Intelligence: Requirements and Features**

Apple Intelligence requires specific hardware, software, and regional conditions [3][4]:

- **Hardware:** Mac with M1 chip or later; iPhone 15 Pro/Pro Max or iPhone 16 series; iPad with M1 or later [3].
- **Software:** macOS 15.1 or later, iOS 18.1 or later, iPadOS 18.1 or later [3].
- **Language:** Device and Siri language must be set to a supported language (English—U.S., U.K., Australia, Canada, and others added progressively) [3][4].
- **Storage:** Minimum 4 GB of on-device storage required for model downloads [3].
- **Regional/Account:** Apple ID must be signed in; the feature must be enabled via Settings > Apple Intelligence & Siri. Available across devices when signed into the same Apple ID [3].

**Key feature categories** include Writing Tools (rewrite, proofread, summarize), Smart Reply, Image Playground, Genmoji, enhanced Siri with on-screen context awareness, notification summaries, and Clean Up in Photos [3][4].

**3. Apple Singapore Online Store Purchase Protections**

Apple's Singapore online store offers a **14-calendar-day return window** from the date of delivery for standard products. Items must be returned in original condition with packaging and accessories [5]. Refunds are processed to the original payment method. Personalized or engraved products, opened software, and electronic gift cards are **non-returnable** [5]. Apple Singapore provides a **price protection policy**: if Apple reduces a product's price within 14 days of purchase, customers may request a refund of the difference [5]. All returns require contacting Apple or initiating through the online order portal.

## References
1. Apple Support, "Mac – Official Apple Support," https://support.apple.com/mac
2. Apple Support Singapore, https://support.apple.com/en-sg
3. Apple Support, "Apple Intelligence," https://support.apple.com/en-us/111901
4. Apple, "Apple Intelligence – Features," https://www.apple.com/apple-intelligence/
5. Apple Singapore, "Shopping Help – Returns & Refunds," https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the core entities (Apple Support ecosystem, Apple Intelligence requirements, and Apple Singapore Online Store terms), so both pass the DEEP logic check. However, on WIDTH/completeness, Agent A omits several required checklist elements: no mention of the Apple Support app or community forums, no detailed cross-device AI compatibility (iPhone Pro models, iPad mini, Vision Pro, Apple Watch pairing), no volume purchase policy (7 days + 25% restocking fee), and no Singapore-specific refund restrictions (Singapore bank account only, no remittance outside Singapore, returns limited to country of purchase). Agent A also understates storage requirements and lacks several regional limitations. Agent B, while still missing some items (e.g., volume purchase rule, Singapore-specific refund remittance restriction, Vision Pro/Watch compatibility, and correct storage figure), is more comprehensive overall and better structured. In terms of user experience, B provides clearer bulleting, better feature categorization, and higher citation density. Since both contain factual gaps (including incorrect storage requirement), the winner cannot receive MUCH_BETTER. Therefore, B wins on breadth and presentation.
